article_title: Federal Judge Blocks Kennedy's Overhaul of U.S. Childhood Vaccine Schedule, Invalidates Reconstituted Advisory Panel

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: edition.cnn.com

content_preview.title: Federal Judge Blocks Kennedy's Overhaul of U.S. Childhood Vaccine Schedule, Invalidates Reconstituted Advisory Panel
content_preview.summary: A federal court has halted sweeping changes to the national childhood immunization schedule and frozen Kennedy's ACIP appointments, finding the reconstituted panel likely violated federal law.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A federal judge in Boston has temporarily blocked Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s sweeping overhaul of the nation's childhood vaccine schedule, ruling that the changes and the reconstituted advisory panel behind them likely violated federal law. The March 16 preliminary injunction, issued by U.S. District Court Judge Brian E. Murphy, freezes a revised immunization schedule that had cut recommended childhood vaccines from 17 diseases to 11 and invalidates ...

editor_notes.source_verification: NBC News confirms Judge Murphy ruling, ACIP firings of 17 experts, schedule reduction (17 to 11 diseases, though NBC says 18 to 11 — minor discrepancy), dropped vaccines including hep A/B, RSV, dengue, meningitis. CBS News confirms Murphy quotes verbatim ('even under the most generous reading...only six'), Dr. Racine quote, Federal Advisory Committee Act violation, injunction effects. HHS appeal confirmed by both sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Minor discrepancy: article says 17 diseases reduced to 11; NBC source says 18 to 11. CBS confirms 17. The discrepancy likely reflects counting methodology. All other facts verified: March 16 ruling date, Judge Brian E. Murphy, AAP + 200+ organizations as plaintiffs, 13 ACIP appointees blocked, June 2025 votes invalidated, hepatitis B newborn recommendation reversal.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Appropriately neutral for a politically sensitive topic. Reports both sides (AAP reaction and HHS appeal plans). No editorializing or advocacy. Quotes are attributed and verified.
editor_notes.originality: Well-organized legal news coverage with clear separation of what changed, what the ruling does, and what remains uncertain. Five strong sources from major outlets.
concerns: Minor discrepancy in disease count (17 vs 18) between article and one source — should be noted but not blocking.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid public health news article at 665 words. Five major news sources provide robust verification. Minor disease count discrepancy noted but not material to the story's accuracy.
